content.full_text:
June 11, 1976

Mr. A. E. Anderson, Secretary
Kingsburg Cemetery District
Post Office Box 542
Kingsburg, California 93631

Dear Mr. Anderson:

This is in response to your inquiry of May 25, 1976.

The Political Reform Act of 1974, Government Code
§87300 et seq., requires the development of Conflict of
Interest Codes by every local government agency. The
deadline for submission of your Cemetery District's Code
is October 10, 1976.

Any person, whether the person was appointed or
elected, holding a position which involves the making
or participation in the making of governmental decisions
which may affect a financial interest, will be subject to
the disclosure and disqualification provisions of his or
her agency's Conflict of Interest Code. As the governing
body of the Cemetery District, the Board of Trustees will
be "designated employees" under the District's Code. Your
Board must determine whether other persons, such as the
Superintendent, should be "designated employees".

Form 721 was developed for use by persons required to
file pursuant to Government Code §87300, and is used by
both elected and appointed officials. It was sent to you
only for guidance in the development of a form for your
district's disclosure statement. You may wish to modify it
to meet the disclosure requirements of your District's Con-
flict of Interest Code, or develop your own form.

I hope this information is helpful. If you have
further questions, do not hesitate to contact the Conflicts
Division.

Sincerely,

Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., Chief
Conflicts of Interest Division

/glb

Kingsburg Cemetery District
P. O. Box 542                                                                    Telephone 897-2426
                                    Office on the Cemetery Grounds
                                    KINGSBURG, CALIFORNIA 93631

                                                                    May 25, 1976


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, Calif. 95814

Gentlemen:

     Thank you for forwarding the required
forms for purpose of filing on or before Oct. 10, 1976.
In reading the Statement of Economic Interest, Form
721, it is noted that this form is for elected officials
only and does not pertain to appointed officials. The
Board of Trustees of this Association, three taxpayers,
in the district are appointed by the Board of Supervisors
of Fresno County for a four year period. The Board of
Trustees of the Kingsburg Cemetery District in turn appoint
the Superintendent and Secretary of the District. It
therefore appears that no officials of this Cemetery
District are required to file the conflict of interest
forms. We therefore wish to have a clarification of
this point before proceeding with completion of the
required forms. Our Cemetery obtains tax funds from
taxpayers in Fresno, Tulare and Kings Counties and
under our interpretation of your instructions the
report, if required by your agency, should be filed
in Sacramento or before Oct. 10, 1976. Other
agencies have indicated the filing deadline as being
August 1, 1976. Please verify which date is correct
and whether or not reports will be required from the
three Trustees and the Superintendent and Secretary
of our Cemetery District. Thank you for sending us
the forms and will appreciate an early answer on the
questions propounded herein.

                                                   Yours very truly,


                                                   A. E. Anderson
AEA:a                                             Secretary
